groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Minimum inputs for a trustworthy product description.

groups.title: Features & benefits
groups.description: Confirmed features, benefits, USPs, objections, limits.

groups.title: Usage & policies
groups.description: How to use, care, safety, warranty, shipping, returns.

variables.label: Product name
variables.placeholder: Creatornivo Pro — content generation for marketers
variables.hint: Exact product name customers will see.

help.why: Anchors titles, opening, and SEO without inventing a different product.
help.example: Creatornivo Free plan
help.avoid: Generic names like “Amazing Product” or unconfirmed model numbers.

variables.label: Product category
variables.placeholder: AI writing tools / content templates

help.why: Shapes benefit hierarchy and which specs matter most.
help.example: SaaS content generation software
help.avoid: Vague categories that do not match how you sell it.

variables.label: Brand name
variables.placeholder: Creatornivo
variables.hint: Brand as shown on the listing.

help.why: Used in brand-oriented titles and consistent voice.
help.example: Creatornivo
help.avoid: Invented brands or multiple conflicting brand names.

variables.label: Product type
variables.placeholder: Software or application
variables.hint: Physical, digital, software, etc. — unlocks type-specific rules.

help.why: Digital vs physical vs food require different specs and claims caution.
help.example: Software or application
help.avoid: Choosing a type that does not match how the product is delivered.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: small-team marketers and founders who publish content

help.why: Adjusts terminology, depth, and use cases.
help.example: Early-stage B2B founders and content leads
help.avoid: Assuming every shopper is an expert or a buyer of everything.

variables.label: Customer problem
variables.placeholder: Hard to write trustworthy product or social copy without inventing claims
variables.hint: Primary need or problem the product addresses.

help.why: Opens the description with real relevance, not empty praise.
help.example: Need structured content templates without fake social proof
help.avoid: Invented pain points the product does not address.

variables.label: Main purpose
variables.placeholder: Generate structured marketing copy from confirmed product facts
variables.hint: What the product is mainly for.

help.why: First paragraph must state purpose clearly.
help.example: Help teams draft platform-ready copy from real inputs
help.avoid: Vague purpose like “improve your life”.

variables.label: Platform
variables.placeholder: E-commerce product page
variables.hint: Where this description will be published.

help.why: Marketplace vs brand store vs B2B need different structures.
help.example: Shopify store
help.avoid: Using one generic structure for every platform.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: clear, specific, helpful, no hype

help.why: Keeps copy persuasive without exaggerated ad language.
help.example: professional, concrete, trustworthy
help.avoid: Revolutionary / game-changing / must-have fluff.

variables.label: Language
variables.placeholder: English

help.why: Titles, bullets, and SEO elements should match this language.
help.example: English
help.avoid: Mixing languages without stating so.

variables.label: Key features
variables.placeholder:
Feature 1…
Feature 2…
(only confirmed features)
variables.hint: Facts about what the product has or does.

help.why: Each important feature should map to a practical benefit.
help.example: 45 structured templates; Free 5/day; Pro 100/month
help.avoid: Unreleased features described as available.

variables.label: Key benefits
variables.placeholder:
Benefit 1…
Benefit 2…
(outcomes customers can expect)
variables.hint: Customer outcomes — not just feature names.

help.why: Benefit hierarchy drives bullets and opening value.
help.example: Faster first drafts; clearer structure; fewer invented claims
help.avoid: Guaranteed results without evidence.

variables.label: Unique selling points
variables.placeholder: What is distinct — only if true and specific

help.why: Supports positioning without unsupported superlatives.
help.example: Honest Early Access messaging; no fake testimonials
help.avoid: Claims like "only product that..." without proof.

variables.label: Competitive advantages
variables.placeholder: Compared to alternatives — only fair, confirmed points
variables.hint: Optional. Do not invent competitor weaknesses.

help.why: Helps positioning when differences are real.
help.example: Template-first workflow with transparent quotas
help.avoid: False comparisons or invented competitor flaws.

variables.label: Customer objections
variables.placeholder: Is it hard to learn? Will it invent claims? Pricing?
variables.hint: Concerns to address only with confirmed answers.

help.why: Enables honest objection handling without fake reassurance.
help.example: Worried AI will invent stats — we only use supplied facts
help.avoid: Making up answers you cannot verify.

variables.label: Product limitations
variables.placeholder: What the product does not do or does not include

help.why: Copy must not hide relevant limitations.
help.example: Does not auto-publish to marketplaces; facts must be supplied
help.avoid: Hiding limits to sound more complete.

variables.label: Materials or ingredients
variables.placeholder: Only confirmed materials or ingredients

help.why: Required for physical, beauty, food, handmade listings when real.
help.example: 100% cotton / leave blank for software
help.avoid: Guessing composition or allergen status.

variables.label: Technical specifications
variables.placeholder: Model, power, OS, file format, capacity…

help.why: Marketplace and technical buyers need scannable facts.
help.example: Web app; English UI; Free and Pro plans
help.avoid: Invented measurements or system requirements.

variables.label: Dimensions
variables.placeholder: e.g. 30 × 20 × 10 cm — only if confirmed

help.why: Fit and shipping decisions depend on real sizes.
help.example: Leave blank if not a physical product
help.avoid: Approximate guesses presented as exact.

variables.label: Weight
variables.placeholder: e.g. 450 g — only if confirmed

help.why: Shipping and usability context.
help.example: e.g. 450 g — only if confirmed
help.avoid: Invented weight.

variables.label: Available sizes
variables.placeholder: S, M, L / One size / N/A

help.why: Clothing and similar products need size clarity.
help.example: S, M, L / One size / N/A
help.avoid: Guessing fit charts.

variables.label: Available variants
variables.placeholder: Colors, finishes, formats — confirmed only

help.why: Helps customers choose the right option.
help.example: Colors, finishes, formats — confirmed only
help.avoid: Listing variants that are not sellable.

variables.label: Compatibility
variables.placeholder: Devices, OS, software, accessories — confirmed only

help.why: Reduces returns and support friction.
help.example: Modern browsers; account required
help.avoid: Assuming cables, adapters, or OS support.

variables.label: Package contents
variables.placeholder: What is in the box / what the purchase includes

help.why: Answers “what do I get?” with confirmed items only.
help.example: What is in the box / what the purchase includes
help.avoid: Assuming accessories are included.

variables.label: How to use
variables.placeholder: Step-by-step usage when known

help.why: Supports How to Use section when instructions exist.
help.example: Step-by-step usage when known
help.avoid: Unsafe or invented instructions.

variables.label: Care instructions
variables.placeholder: Wash, store, maintain — only if confirmed

help.why: Physical and textile products need accurate care notes.
help.example: Wash, store, maintain — only if confirmed
help.avoid: Generic care advice that may damage the product.

variables.label: Safety information
variables.placeholder: Warnings, age limits, hazards — confirmed only

help.why: High-stakes categories need supplied warnings only.
help.example: Warnings, age limits, hazards — confirmed only
help.avoid: Inventing medical/safety claims or disclaimers.

variables.label: Certifications
variables.placeholder: CE, organic, ISO… only if verified

help.why: Trust signals only when real.
help.example: CE, organic, ISO… only if verified
help.avoid: Claiming approval without confirmation.

variables.label: Warranty
variables.placeholder: e.g. 12-month limited warranty — or leave blank

help.why: Purchase confidence from confirmed terms.
help.example: e.g. 12-month limited warranty — or leave blank
help.avoid: Invented warranty periods.

variables.label: Shipping information
variables.placeholder: Delivery method, regions, times — confirmed only

help.why: Physical and digital delivery differ — only state what is true.
help.example: Delivery method, regions, times — confirmed only
help.avoid: Fake delivery promises; digital products as shipped goods.

variables.label: Returns information
variables.placeholder: Return window and conditions if known

help.why: Addresses a common objection with real policy text.
help.example: Return window and conditions if known
help.avoid: Invented return rights.

variables.label: Price
variables.placeholder: e.g. Free tier; Pro $X/month — confirmed only

help.why: Purchase context when pricing is public and correct.
help.example: e.g. Free tier; Pro $X/month — confirmed only
help.avoid: Guessed prices or fake discounts.

variables.label: Offer
variables.placeholder: Only confirmed promo — no fake scarcity

help.why: Includes offer only when real; no invented deadlines.
help.example: Only confirmed promo — no fake scarcity
help.avoid: False urgency or limited stock claims.

variables.label: Call to action
variables.placeholder: Add to cart / View plans / Choose your option
variables.hint: One primary CTA — not aggressive scarcity.

help.why: Ends the package with one clear next step for the platform.
help.example: View plans and start generating
help.avoid: Buy now before it’s too late / Do not miss out.

variables.label: Seo keywords
variables.placeholder: primary keyword; secondary, secondary…

help.why: Optional natural inclusion in titles and meta; readability first.
help.example: AI product description generator; ecommerce copy
help.avoid: Irrelevant keywords or unsupported claims for SEO.

variables.label: Length
variables.placeholder: medium
variables.hint: Editorial depth for the main description.

help.why: Controls depth without padding a simple product.
help.example: medium
help.avoid: Making a simple item artificially long.

variables.label: Additional context
variables.placeholder: Restrictions, must-avoid claims, brand rules, legal notes…
variables.hint: Anything else the model must respect.

help.why: Captures claim bans and brand rules so copy stays accurate.
help.example: Do not invent reviews or ratings. No medical claims.
help.avoid: Leaving critical restrictions only in your head.
